title: Gooey Smoky Peruvian Shrimp Ceviche
description: A quick, briny shrimp ceviche with smoky flavor and creamy avocado, perfect for a light snack or appetizer.
instructions: Pat the shrimp dry with paper towels and cut them into bite‑sized pieces.
Place the shrimp in a shallow glass bowl and pour the lime juice over them, ensuring all pieces are submerged.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 5 minutes to lightly cure the shrimp.
While the shrimp marinate, thinly slice the red onion and dice the tomato.
Remove the shrimp from the refrigerator and drain about one‑third of the lime juice, leaving enough liquid to keep the mixture moist.
Add the sliced onion, diced tomato, chopped cilantro, and diced avocado to the bowl.
Stir in the smoked paprika, sea salt, black pepper, and olive oil until all ingredients are evenly coated.
Adjust the seasoning with extra salt or pepper if needed, and optionally add a dash of hot sauce for heat.
Serve the ceviche immediately in chilled bowls or on toasted quinoa for a crunchy contrast.
Garnish with a few sprigs of cilantro and a squeeze of lime before serving.
